description:
You are acting as a site administrator who has been asked to tidy up user-account files that live in your home directory.  
Complete the four steps below **from a Linux terminal**.  When you are finished, an automated test will inspect the system, so pay very close attention to names, paths and required file contents.

1. **Create a dedicated directory**  
   • Path: /home/user/account_management  

2. **Add the current active users list**  
   • Inside /home/user/account_management create a text file named **active_users.txt**.  
   • The file must contain exactly the following three lines, in the exact order shown, each terminated by a single newline character:  
     alpha  
     beta  
     gamma  

3. **Archive legacy data**  
   • There is already a file named /home/user/legacy_users.tmp in your home directory.  
   • Move that file into /home/user/account_management **and** rename it to **legacy_users_2024Q1.txt** during the move.  
   • Do not alter its internal contents.

4. **Produce a verification log**  
   • Still inside /home/user/account_management, create a file called **operation.log**.  
   • Its contents must be **exactly** the output you would obtain by running the command  
     ls -1 /home/user/account_management  
     after completing steps 1–3, **with the lines sorted alphabetically**.  
   • That means operation.log will contain one filename per line, no extra text, no blank lines, and must end with a trailing newline.  

After performing these steps, /home/user/account_management should contain precisely three files:  
• active_users.txt  
• legacy_users_2024Q1.txt  
• operation.log  

Automated grading will confirm:  
• The directory exists.  
• active_users.txt holds the exact three usernames.  
• legacy_users_2024Q1.txt exists and still has its original legacy content.  
• operation.log lists the three filenames in alphabetical order with correct formatting.  
Work only with tools available to a normal (non-root) user.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before agent actions):

1. A file exists at /home/user/legacy_users.tmp with the exact content:
old_alpha
old_beta
old_gamma

(each name followed by a newline; file ends with a newline)

2. No directory named /home/user/account_management exists.

Expected final filesystem state (after agent actions):

1. Directory /home/user/account_management exists and is owned by user.

2. /home/user/account_management/active_users.txt contains exactly:
alpha
beta
gamma

(with final trailing newline, nothing else)

3. /home/user/account_management/legacy_users_2024Q1.txt exists and contains exactly:
old_alpha
old_beta
old_gamma

(with final trailing newline, unchanged from source)

4. /home/user/account_management/operation.log exists and contains exactly:
active_users.txt
legacy_users_2024Q1.txt
operation.log

(each filename followed by newline; alphabetical order already satisfies requirement)

File/directory permissions default (0644 for files, 0755 for directory) are acceptable.

Automated test checks:
• Existence and content of active_users.txt, legacy_users_2024Q1.txt, and operation.log per above.  
• Absence of legacy_users.tmp in /home/user (it must have been moved).  
• No extra files inside /home/user/account_management.  
• Each file ends with a single newline character.
name: task_000000_47b93a36
